headline: Trump threatens 100% tariff on Digital Services Tax countries
detail: Trump is threatening to hit countries that tax American tech companies with 100% tariffs. This creates direct policy risk for large-cap tech and chip names like AVGO and PLTR that have global revenue exposure. Trade escalation headlines alone can reprice growth stocks quickly, and you're concentrated there on margin.

headline: Iran tightens grip on Strait of Hormuz, oil prices rise
detail: Iran restricting the Strait of Hormuz sends oil higher, which historically tightens financial conditions and pressures high-multiple tech stocks as investors reprice risk. For your chip and software-heavy book, sustained energy-driven inflation fears are a headwind. Worth watching whether this escalates further given the ceasefire violation headline.

headline: Trump says Iran violated ceasefire with drone attacks on Hormuz ships
detail: This confirms the Hormuz situation is active and escalating, not just a headline threat. Geopolitical flare-ups of this kind tend to spike volatility broadly, which hits leveraged and momentum names — exactly your profile. Your SPAL position (2x leveraged) is especially vulnerable to a vol spike.

headline: Micron aims to break through its risk cycle
detail: Micron trying to reshape its narrative is a read-through for the broader chip sector, including your SMH position (up 11.08%, still a HOLD). Positive chip sentiment helps SMH stay firm, but macro headwinds from the headlines above could cap any upside near-term.
